entries.title: Computer Science Admission Requirements in Canada
entries.content: For many Computer Science programs in Canada, common expectations include a strong academic record, GPA around 3.0 to 3.7 (or equivalent), and IELTS around 6.5 to 7.0. Exact requirements vary by university and by undergraduate vs postgraduate level.

entries.title: IELTS Guidance for Competitive Programs
entries.content: Many competitive programs ask for IELTS 6.5 to 7.0 overall. Some may require stronger writing or speaking bands. Students should always verify department-level requirements before applying.

entries.title: Scholarships and Funding
entries.content: Common options include merit-based entrance scholarships, need-based support, and program-specific grants. Applying early and submitting complete documents usually improves scholarship outcomes.

entries.title: Application Timeline
entries.content: A practical timeline is to start 8 to 10 months before intake. Test preparation and document collection may take 1 to 2 months, and admissions decisions can take several weeks after submission.

entries.title: Career Prospects After Graduation
entries.content: Graduates commonly pursue software engineering, data roles, cloud engineering, and security positions. Career outcomes depend on technical skills, internships, portfolio quality, and communication skills.

entries.title: Evaluating Value for Money
entries.content: To evaluate value for money, compare tuition, living cost, scholarship availability, internship/co-op opportunities, and post-study work outcomes, not tuition alone.
